message:
fix(reactor): harden modify/deregister for stale fd cleanup, add kqueue fd-reuse safety

epoll.rs:
- Harden modify() to handle stale registrations: when the kernel returns ENOENT
  or EBADF (fd closed since registration), clean up both token and fd maps so
  fd-number reuse is not blocked indefinitely by stale bookkeeping entries. The
  previous modify() would propagate the raw error leaving stale entries behind
- Extract raw_fd before mutable borrow to satisfy the borrow checker when
  cleaning up on error paths
- Add comprehensive test (modify_closed_fd_cleans_stale_bookkeeping_for_fd_reuse)
  that verifies: modify on closed fd returns NotFound, stale bookkeeping is
  removed, and a new socket dup2'd onto the same fd number can be registered
  and receives events correctly

kqueue.rs:
- Add duplicate fd registration check in register() — the epoll reactor already
  had this via the fd reverse index, but kqueue was missing it, allowing the
  same fd to be registered under two tokens which causes ambiguous event routing
- Add fd validity check (fcntl F_GETFD) before registration to fail fast on
  closed fds instead of getting confusing kernel errors later
- Make deregister() best-effort for closed fds: handle ENOENT (already removed
  from kqueue) and EBADF (fd closed) by cleaning up bookkeeping and returning
  Ok, matching the epoll reactor's behavior. Previously these cases returned
  errors, leaving stale registration entries
- Rename test from deregister_closed_fd_returns_error to
  deregister_closed_fd_is_best_effort reflecting the corrected semantics
- Add reused_fd_cannot_register_under_new_token_until_stale_token_removed test
  mirroring the epoll reactor's equivalent test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
